message:
Fix Safari caching OAuth redirects by adding Cache-Control: no-store (#3454)

Safari aggressively caches 307 redirects when no Cache-Control header is
present. The /auth/github endpoint returns a 307 with a unique CSRF state
parameter — when Safari caches this, subsequent login attempts reuse a stale
state that the server already consumed, causing CSRF validation to fail.

Chrome does not cache 307 redirects, which is why this only affected Safari.

Signed-off-by: clubanderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
